name: TinaCMS Git-Based Headless CMS API
what_it_does: Git-based headless CMS with GraphQL API — enabling development teams to add visual content editing to Next.js, Gatsby, and Nuxt sites with content stored directly in Git (Markdown, MDX, JSON), a contextual visual editor for editors to edit content in context, and Tina Cloud for managed Git sync and editorial workflow without exposing Git to editors.
use_cases: Adding visual in-context editing to Next.js or Gatsby sites via TinaCMS integration
Querying Markdown and MDX content via TinaCMS GraphQL API for statically generated sites
Managing structured content (blog posts, pages, data files) via TinaCMS API for content automation
Building editorial workflows where editors edit via visual UI with content stored in Git
Integrating TinaCMS with CI/CD pipelines via webhook for automated builds on content changes
not_for: Enterprise DXP with marketing automation (TinaCMS is developer CMS, not marketing platform)
Non-Git-based content storage (TinaCMS requires Git as content backend)
Complex editorial workflows with approval chains (use Contentful or Storyblok for enterprise workflows)
best_when: A Next.js or Gatsby development team wants to give non-technical editors a visual editing experience while keeping content in Git (Markdown/MDX) — without building a separate CMS or exposing Git to editors.
avoid_when: You need complex editorial workflows, multi-region content delivery CDN, enterprise DXP features, or non-developers can't tolerate any Git-adjacent complexity.

auth.notes: Client ID and read-only token for content delivery. Tina Cloud for managed editorial access (editors authenticate via GitHub OAuth). GraphQL API for content queries and mutations. Webhook for content change events via Tina Cloud. Open source self-hostable (no cloud required for read-only). JavaScript/TypeScript SDK (tinacms package). No REST API — GraphQL only. GitHub, GitLab, or self-hosted Git as content backend.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: open source self-hosted; Tina Cloud free for 1 user
pricing.paid_tiers: Tina Cloud Team $29/month (3 editors); Business $99/month (10 editors); Scale $299/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Vancouver company. Founded 2019 by Scott Byrne-Fraser (formerly Forestry.io). Raised $5M seed. Open source project (Apache 2.0) with Tina Cloud as managed service. GitHub-native with strong Next.js community integration. 10,000+ GitHub stars. Originally Forest Admin / Forestry.io pivot. Visual editing in context (inline editing) is the key differentiator vs. form-based headless CMS. Tina Cloud handles Git auth so editors don't need Git accounts. Growing Next.js ecosystem adoption.

performance.rate_limits: GraphQL API rate limits per Tina Cloud tier

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: GraphQL API for content queries and mutations. API key/client ID auth. Good documentation with Next.js and Gatsby examples. Webhook via Tina Cloud for content events. Open source self-hosting option for read-only. GraphQL schema auto-generated from content model definition. Canadian company with GDPR compliance. Rate limits per cloud tier.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Content reads fully idempotent. Document reads idempotent by path. GraphQL queries idempotent. Mutations use file path as natural idempotency key.
known_agent_gotchas: GraphQL only (no REST) — agents must use GraphQL query pattern
Content mutations go through Git — may take seconds for content to be available after commit
Schema auto-generation requires content model definition in tina/config.ts
Tina Cloud required for editor authentication — self-hosted requires custom auth implementation
Git as content backend means content changes create Git commits — audit trail by design

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR compliant. US and self-hosted data residency. API key auth. Open source codebase allows security audit. Git-backed content provides natural audit trail. Read token must be protected (gives read access to all content). Write token for mutations must never be client-side exposed. GitHub OAuth for editor authentication via Tina Cloud. Self-hosted eliminates cloud data residency concerns.
